provenance: Fixture for Change 1 — _collect_sim_returns date-labeling and cscv_date_returns user_attr persistence. All structural properties are derived from the CPCV partition invariant: CPCV path dates are contiguous, non-overlapping partitions of the eligible window, so the UNION of (date, return) pairs across paths covers each date AT MOST ONCE. This is a shape/invariant fixture — no dollar/pct values from the producer are hardcoded; assertions check structure (unique dates, only triggered days present, dates within eligible window) not specific return magnitudes.

partition_invariant.description: CPCV folds partition the eligible dates into non-overlapping groups. Each of the C(N_groups, K_test) test paths covers a different subset of groups, so no date appears in two paths' test sets simultaneously. The union of per-path {date: guard_alpha} dicts therefore has NO collision — the 'union covers eligible window once' property holds by construction.
partition_invariant.consequence: The cscv_date_returns user_attr, built as the UNION of per-path (date, return) pairs, must: (1) have len <= len(eligible_dates), (2) have no duplicate date keys, (3) contain only dates within the eligible window, (4) contain only TRIGGERED days (triggered_return is not None).

no_triggered_returns.description: A simulation where no day triggers an exit. cscv_date_returns must be an empty dict {}.

single_triggered_day.description: One triggered day. cscv_date_returns must contain exactly one key.
single_triggered_day.assertion: is positive float (guard_alpha is triggered_return - eod_return)

multi_path_union_no_collision.description: Two paths with non-overlapping test dates. The union must have both paths' triggered dates with no overwrites. This directly tests the partition-invariant property: if path A covers [2024-01-01..2024-01-05] and path B covers [2024-01-06..2024-01-10], the union has all triggered dates from both without collision.

dates_within_eligible_window_only.description: Verifies that cscv_date_returns contains NO dates outside the eligible window (sorted_dates[:frozen_start_idx]). The frozen eval fold dates must not appear.
dates_within_eligible_window_only.rule: all(date in eligible_dates for date in cscv_date_returns.keys())
